name: Goo Goo Dolls
description: Goo Goo Dolls are an American rock band from Buffalo, New York, formed in 1986 by guitarist/vocalist John Rzeznik and bassist/vocalist Robby Takac. After years of underground punk and alt-rock releases, they broke through in the mid-1990s with the single 'Name' and achieved massive mainstream success with 'Iris,' which spent 11 consecutive months on the Billboard Hot 100 and topped Billboard's 'Top 100 Pop Songs 1992-2012' chart.
trivia: The band's name was inspired by an ad for a 'Goo Goo Doll' toy that Rzeznik spotted in a True Detective magazine.
'Iris' spent 18 consecutive weeks at #1 on the Billboard Hot 100 Airplay chart -- the longest run in the chart's history at the time.

Despite 'Iris' being one of the biggest songs of the 1990s, it was never officially released as a commercial single during its initial run, making it ineligible for the Billboard Hot 100's top spot under rules at the time.
The band has sold over 15 million records worldwide.
John Rzeznik was inducted into the Songwriters Hall of Fame in 2008.
Rzeznik and Takac originally performed under the name 'The Sex Maggots' before a venue owner refused to put the name on the marquee, prompting the change.
The band has received four Grammy Award nominations throughout their career.
